name: Mari El (Russia)

colors.symbolism: purity and the Mari national colour
the traditional colour of Mari embroidery
earth and the Mari heraldic bear
the sun and the wheat sheaves
outline detailing of the coat of arms

description: A white field with a vertical strip of red Mari national ornament along the hoist; the coat of arms of Mari El, featuring a bear with a sword and shield, is placed in the centre.
history: Adopted on 1 June 2011, replacing the 2006 blue-white-crimson tricolour. The redesign drew controversy as it diminished the role of traditional Mari symbolism.

features.symbolism: a white field bordered along the hoist by a vertical strip of traditional Mari embroidery; the coat of arms in the centre identifies the republic.
a vertical strip of traditional Mari national ornament running along the hoist.

features.role: coat of arms of Mari El
features.symbolism: a heraldic bear holding a sword and shield, surrounded by oak and ear-of-wheat branches, over the rising sun, representing the strength and identity of the Mari people.
